content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string S=S1S2…SNS = S_1S_2 \ldots S_NS=S1​S2​…SN​ of length NNN. You are also given an integer KKK. You can do the following operation at most N−K+1N-K+1N−K+1 times:


First, choose a substring SiSi+1…Si+K−1S_iS_{i+1} \ldots S_{i+K-1}Si​Si+1​…Si+K−1​ of SSS of length exactly KKK. This substring shouldn't have been chosen in any of the previous operations. That is, the same starting index of the substring can't be chosen twice.
Then, flip all the characters of the chosen substring (i.e. change every 000 to 111 and vice versa).
Find the lexicographically smallest string possible after the operations if you do them in an optimal way.

**Note**:


A substring is consecutive elements of a string. For eg. in the string "01011", "101" is a substring, but "111" is not a substring.
A string AAA is lexicographically smaller than string BBB, if Ai<BiA_i < B_iAi​<Bi​, where iii is the first index where AAA and BBB differ.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers: NNN, KKK.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the lexicographically smallest string that you can achieve.
constraints: S consists only of characters '0' and '1'
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains a binary string S of length N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the lexicographically smallest binary string that can be obtained after performing at most N‑K+1 operations as described.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The greedy algorithm flips a substring whenever the current effective character is '1' and a flip can still be started, producing the smallest possible strings.
Starting flips at positions 0,2 and 4 turns all bits to 0, which is lexicographically minimal.

solution.approach: We scan the string from left to right keeping the parity of the number of flips that affect the current position. A difference array diff[] stores where a previously started flip ends: when we start a flip at i we toggle the current parity and set diff[i+K] ^= 1 so that after K positions the effect is cancelled. At position i the effective bit is (S[i] XOR cur). If this bit is 1 and we are still allowed to start a flip (i <= N‑K) we start a flip – this makes the current bit 0 and is always optimal because any later operation cannot change an earlier character without making it larger lexicographically. The algorithm therefore produces the smallest possible string. The scan is O(N) and uses O(N) extra memory for diff.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary array (diff) per test case

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;\npublic class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int K=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());String S=br.readLine().trim();int[] diff=new int[N+2];int cur=0;StringBuilder ans=new StringBuilder(N);for(int i=0;i<N;++i){cur ^= diff[i];int bit=(S.charAt(i)-'0') ^ cur; if(bit==1 && i<=N-K){cur ^= 1; diff[i+K] ^= 1; bit=0;} ans.append((char)('0'+bit));}out.append(ans).append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it)); K=int(next(it))
        S=next(it)
        diff=[0]*(N+2)
        cur=0
        ans=[]
        for i,ch in enumerate(S):
            cur ^= diff[i]
            bit=(ord(ch)-48) ^ cur
            if bit==1 and i<=N-K:
                cur ^= 1
                diff[i+K] ^= 1
                bit=0
            ans.append('0' if bit==0 else '1')
        out.append(''.join(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('
'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
code.JavaScript: const fs=require('fs');\\nconst input=fs.readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split(/\\s+/);\\nlet idx=0;\\nconst T=Number(input[idx++]);\\nlet outputs=[];\\nfor(let t=0;t<T;++t){\\n  const N=Number(input[idx++]);\\n  const K=Number(input[idx++]);\\n  const S=input[idx++];\\n  const diff=new Uint8Array(N+2);\\n  let cur=0;\\n  let ans='';\\n  for(let i=0;i<N;++i){\\n    cur ^= diff[i];\\n    let bit=(S[i]==='1'?1:0) ^ cur;\\n    if(bit===1 && i<=N-K){\\n      cur ^= 1;\\n      diff[i+K] ^= 1;\\n      bit=0;\\n    }\\n    ans += bit===1?'1':'0';\\n  }\\n  outputs.push(ans);\\n}\\nconsole.log(outputs.join('\\n'));
